payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Those who don't recognise my costume, you're really confused, especially if this is your first time tuning in. Sorry. (normal voice) I am not dungeon mastering like that for the rest of the evening because I will have to run into traffic after an hour. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (as Victor) Welcome to tonight's episode of Critical Role, our Halloween edition. Where a bunch of us nerdy-ass voice actors sit around and play Dungeons & Dragons. Hi. Tonight we will astound you with spooky tales. I don't know what's going to happen as we are just going to continue the game that we normally play. Welcome, I'm Matthew Mercer, dungeon master. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, you take a short rest to set your-- (laughs).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, for the restoring some of the farmland, yeah. Which as you complete the entirety of the spell, you watch as the rest of the snow melt begins to turn to water, and you watch as the land itself grows more fertile and the crops begin to pop forth. Like you began to see when the ritual initiated itself. With the completion of the land cleansed, you are free to go to the next length of your journey.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Stop, I can't make eye contact. I'm sorry, it's okay. We'll get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think you look great, Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You look like Doc Brown exploded, I don't know what to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pat that tuft of hair down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Marty! Okay. Happy Halloween.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 30 more minutes, 30 more minutes and we'll settle down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You have to get immune to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would like to set the Deathwalker's Ward to Matthew's costume.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, you take a short rest to set your-- (laughs).

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, for the restoring some of the farmland, yeah. Which as you complete the entirety of the spell, you watch as the rest of the snow melt begins to turn to water, and you watch as the land itself grows more fertile and the crops begin to pop forth. Like you began to see when the ritual initiated itself. With the completion of the land cleansed, you are free to go to the next length of your journey.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Stop, I can't make eye contact. I'm sorry, it's okay. We'll get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think you look great, Matt.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You look like Doc Brown exploded, I don't know what to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pat that tuft of hair down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Marty! Okay. Happy Halloween.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 30 more minutes, 30 more minutes and we'll settle down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You have to get immune to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would like to set the Deathwalker's Ward to Matthew's costume.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you focus and touch the outside of the tree, you watch as this small, familiar portal begins to tear its way through the outer bark of the nearby trunk. From there, as you reach through the magic creating this portal between you and Whitestone, you hear the voice echo in the distance, "I hear you." And thus, the temporary doorway between here and Whitestone is open. The rest of you guys rush through, with Kima and Pike in tow, you make your way into Whitestone itself. At this point in time, it looks like it's fairly late in the afternoon. It's still day here, that's a day, eight hours there. Actually no, it would be pretty early morning by here. At this point, you step out and the sky is starting to come to light. You see the blue slowly transitioning into a light orange to a yellow as the sunrise is making its way over the mountain range that surrounds the outside of Whitestone. You're met with a few wandering guards that wander through the town that acknowledge your presence, but it is your call as to what you wish to do now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean we got to go back to Whitestone, right away? Is there anything else to do here? Is there anything to deliver, any piece of any body to take with us? Anything? We got everything already?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to deliver Ripley's arm back in Ank'Harel.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That needs to happen.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kashaw's in Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to do something about him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He might be in Whitestone by now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He might be back. He might be on his way here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's a dick, he can take care of himself.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hopefully, he won't just loose something on the Ravenites here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's get out of here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They've been through so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean it's really reconfirming his whole life-view so, you know. Hi!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hi!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm having trouble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Keyleth! Cast your thing and let's get the fuck out of here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's get on to Whitestone. Get on getting on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm coming, boo.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "We have a meeting set here in the next two hours to gather our information and best situate the next plan of action. You also have a friend waiting for you. Your armored Kashaw returned but a half day ago apparently. Sleeping at the moment should you be interested in waking him from his slumber and notifying him of his, well, his services no longer being needed."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's probably a charm spell. Or a memory spell. No, I don't want to fucking wait--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not a Counterspell, but you can try to cast it on her to see if it has any effect.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, so the DC of the spell-- okay. So, as you pull back and begin chanting to yourself, clutching Kima and pulling her away from Asum, Asum's grip leaves Kima's shoulder and Kima's still shaking her head groggily. You finish the Dispel Magic and you feel the strange, warm pulse of energy radiate briefly out from Kima's body. Kima turns to look at you and goes, "What-- why would-- I'm sorry, I went groggy there for a second. We've had a long day. Good to see you, Asum." And Asum nods and says, "Well, the pleasure is mine. Glad to see you all made it back safe and sound."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes we did. Thank you, Asum.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Until the meeting. Now, food should be ready." And Asum steps out into the sunshine and closes his eyes, takes a deep breath as the sun rays hit and looks over his shoulder, smiles, and continues to walk down the path in towards Whitestone itself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We have a meeting set here in the next two hours to gather our information and best situate the next plan of action. You also have a friend waiting for you. Your armored Kashaw returned but a half day ago apparently. Sleeping at the moment should you be interested in waking him from his slumber and notifying him of his, well, his services no longer being needed."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did he return alone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He did. And rather proud with his findings."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. Where are you off to now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, I'm here waiting for the meeting."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lovely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And who all is going to be a part of this meeting?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "As far as I know, it's going to be myself, Cassandra, Allura, and Drake."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good! Allura, we haven't seen her in forever. Have we?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We saw her pretty recently.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We did. Shit. Yes, we did. Never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Well then, until the meeting?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It probably should be attended to in a short amount of time, like in the next week or so, before it's really--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, well, we like to talk.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And even if we do, even if we were to get rid of her, there's no sense in believing that they still don't know everything we're doing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Then we'll see what he proposes and take it from there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And let's hope that we're downstairs, and if we're not downstairs, there's still options.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you've finally caught up with the group. You've managed to drop off your large amount of dragon hide in return.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit, that skin weighs a ton. Good thing I'm strong as fuck. What'd I miss?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is there a cold place in the treasure room? Wait, this hide is going to be cured, or smoked, or something, whatever you do. Stretched?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's got scales all over it. I mean that stays, right? It doesn't have to be kept cold?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I mean, it probably should be.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Kept cold.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A few moments pass, no response. (Mario coin sound)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we're not (death noises and farts)?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not just yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're going to talk, we're going to listen, and be prepared for anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Was that last sound the sound of Seeker Asum's bowels?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's open to interpretation.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. After he dies, letting it all out?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just want to be fancy. I lean that way but, you know. Cannot speak for other people.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The work speaks for itself, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fair enough. Vax, as you rush down towards the homestead that Gilmore had made here in Whitestone, the door is closed. There's a large pile out front of ruined furniture that's been jammed into this collection. Presumably from the last encounter they had when the incursion of assassins made their way in. But the door's closed, and seemingly locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (knocks)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) "I certainly hope so. It's taken a couple years off my lifespan."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why did I do this? Tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Now you know why.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, this is not fair. Can I come in to talk? Quickly, I have to head back to the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Would you like some tea?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I don't even have time, I just want to talk to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'd like some tea. Come on in, come on in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He ushers you to enter, and leads you to one of the small chairs. And he walks back into the kitchen, shouting over his shoulder, "So! Vax'ildan, please. Tell me what's on your mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, we're getting closer to the end game here.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Making a name for yourself. I'm proud of you." A few moments pass, and silence. And Gilmore walks back out, clutching a small tray with a few teacups. And as he's walking, he's twirling his finger and you can see a little spiral of flame encircling the tea kettle as it begins to heat up. He brings it over, sets it down on the table. Pulls his robe in and sits down across from you on this low slung tea table. Takes a bit and sips it. "So, what brings you here this early in the morning, friend?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why did I do this? Tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Now you know why.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, this is not fair. Can I come in to talk? Quickly, I have to head back to the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Would you like some tea?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I don't even have time, I just want to talk to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'd like some tea. Come on in, come on in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He ushers you to enter, and leads you to one of the small chairs. And he walks back into the kitchen, shouting over his shoulder, "So! Vax'ildan, please. Tell me what's on your mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, we're getting closer to the end game here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "I certainly hope so. It's taken a couple years off my lifespan."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We just killed another one of the dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Really?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How many is that for you now? Three?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is three.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Making a name for yourself. I'm proud of you." A few moments pass, and silence. And Gilmore walks back out, clutching a small tray with a few teacups. And as he's walking, he's twirling his finger and you can see a little spiral of flame encircling the tea kettle as it begins to heat up. He brings it over, sets it down on the table. Pulls his robe in and sits down across from you on this low slung tea table. Takes a bit and sips it. "So, what brings you here this early in the morning, friend?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why did I do this? Tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Now you know why.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, this is not fair. Can I come in to talk? Quickly, I have to head back to the others.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Would you like some tea?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. I don't even have time, I just want to talk to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'd like some tea. Come on in, come on in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He ushers you to enter, and leads you to one of the small chairs. And he walks back into the kitchen, shouting over his shoulder, "So! Vax'ildan, please. Tell me what's on your mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know, we're getting closer to the end game here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "I certainly hope so. It's taken a couple years off my lifespan."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We just killed another one of the dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Really?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How many is that for you now? Three?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is three.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Shh. That's far too fatalistic for the Vax'ildan that I-- number one. Number two, you have nothing to apologize for. Look, everything's on fire around us all the time. We all jump at whatever bit of comfort finds its way before us. And at the same time, I appreciate your honesty. And I'm thankful for the connection that we do have. And yes, heartbreak comes. But heartbreak goes. It's part of life. It just means that I need to find somebody who's a little more my speed."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think I might be dead in a couple of days. So I came here to apologize. If you think I have no feelings for you, you're kidding yourself. I care for you very deeply. I have for a long time. And I don't think I've treated you fairly. But I wasn't playing games. Shaun. It's been a little weird lately, and I understand why, but I want you to know that I love you as a dear, dear friend, and I really don't think I'm going to be here in a few days. And I would hate to think that I would be--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She gives you a very keen look. And you can see Cassandra. As much as she is the healthiest you've seen her physically, her eyes have dark circles over them. It looks like she's been very harried by the events of these past few weeks. Understandably so. But she takes and looks over the sheet and goes, "Well, very well. Should be an interesting meeting then."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Indeed. That's what our meeting is about."

preceding_turns.speaker: IPHONE
preceding_turns.text: What was that, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What was that, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Sorry, I need to go fire some of my guards. They're making a nuisance. Continue, please."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Two more to go. I fear that it's possible that Whitestone itself may eventually be a target of an attack. I feel it may be inevitable.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That is, actually, what our meeting is about. Prevent that from happening."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Quickly to check. I would know what languages Cassandra spoke, correct?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What languages do you know?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Elven, Celestial, Common.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She knows Elven and Common. She does not know Celestial.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was more your hobby.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I figured. That was my guess. I wanted to confirm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I hand her a small slip of paper that I have written in Elven: "Be prepared for anything today. Keep silent."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, it's early morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can handle a lot of things.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You are a strange gnome."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Takes one to know one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Does not work in this circumstance but I applaud your attempt. It was good. I'll do what I can. But don't forget you have more than one reason to stick around."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know. That's what worries me. I'll think about what you said. Maybe I'll go have a toss, see if that helps, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Here, tell you what. Come here."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And he suckerpunches you in the face. You unexpectedly fall back. You feel the pulsing of the blood run to your cheek. Your eyesight goes white for a second. He goes, "See, now you have to live to return the favor."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You! That was clever. I will definitely get you back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good. I look forward to it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Watch your back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I always do."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Get me some drugs!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Awesome. Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's daytime, right?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Gilmore pops up again and says, "Exactly. We were poring over that thing for a number of weeks, until we had to build this barrier. And to be perfectly honest, I am sick of it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to offer yourself as bait?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If need be. I'm not saying that's the right plan, but I'm saying it is one thing we can perhaps utilize in our plan, in our strategy."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is his hate for you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think-- There was a ship in Draconia, a northern ship of some kind. I still feel there may be some sort of weapon inside it that may prove useful. It was a ship that had been dragged there by the white dragon. It's been bothering me. And then of course we have whatever it is we have downstairs. I'm not saying we should use it, but it is powerful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know how we would use it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can't move it, can we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can't touch it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well. The design and the development and enchantment of it are powerful enough to anchor itself to the soul of a dragon of Thordak's strength. Better part of six months."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's one way."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's another?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Distract his allies and go straight for the heart. You have allies, like Allura just said, all throughout the world."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is true. We have the Herd, we can go get the werewolves.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Ravenites would help.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Everyone who we ask, everyone who we send in there, we're sending in to their death.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is war.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but it's either death now or death a little later.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're sending ourselves as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So, are we talking about sending everyone we know and everything we have to the outskirts of town--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: For a full frontal attack. While we bamf into the dragon's lair.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. We create a massive fucking clusterfuck on the edge of his periphery and we go for the center.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long did it take you, Allura, to create that heart?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Cassandra sits up a little bit in her seat and goes, "If we are smart about this, we can minimize the casualties far more now than they would be a week, two weeks, a month, a year from now."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. We create a massive fucking clusterfuck on the edge of his periphery and we go for the center.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long did it take you, Allura, to create that heart?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. The design and the development and enchantment of it are powerful enough to anchor itself to the soul of a dragon of Thordak's strength. Better part of six months."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Six months.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've probably got a week or two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And that was with a dozen other hands helping me. Most of the Arcana Pansophical was at my side for that and we don't have that kind of time."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we take a day or two and we amass every heavy fucking hitter we have, and we do this. What other choice do we have?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I keep thinking back to Emon where we watched half of our friends die in a second.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A lot of us are going to die.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Everyone's still standing at this point, looking. Cassandra slowly sits down but is still looking between Raishan and Percival. You watch as Asum's form steps towards the center of the room, the flash of green flaring in the eyes with a smile, "The anchor that you speak of was indeed removed from the Fire Elemental Plane. We discovered that it was indestructible. Good on you, Allura. Well created. We tried many different ways: rending it, crushing it, sending it elsewhere without the enchantment tearing Thordak with it. To no avail. In trying to find a way to bring Thordak through, we needed to bring the soul anchor and the Fire Elemental Plane with it. The one place where such research could be gathered promptly, fully, was Pyrah. Now, mind you, it was not my intent for such destruction to rain upon them. I spent many years with them. I got to know them. They're a fine people. In that time, I learned the ways of temporal travel between dimensions. The details that allowed me to see the fabric of the Fire Plane, to where we could encase a bit of it within the soul anchor itself. Once we had managed to crack that essence, I was able to, from a distance, using the allies at the other end-- Thordak had found friendship with some of the ousted fire giants. They found comfort outside of the ordning under the shadow of the fire dragon. In using their deep, rich-blooded past and forging and making, they were able to convert the soul anchor into something that housed the essence of the Fire Plane. However, we didn't have a means of creating a hole large enough to bring both through without destroying both in the process. And as such, we waited for the right time: The Winter's Crest."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Now we can have a real conversation about what's going on. We have an ally on the inside, albeit an untrustworthy one. You'll pardon that, but there's every possibility that this is an elaborate trap. I respect your intelligence far too much to try and pretend otherwise. You understand certainly what I mean? Having this on the table, we now know that there's every possibility that the red dragon knows everything. There's every possibility that we're walking into a trap. Or there's every possibility that we have an insatiably powerful ally. It is a terrible risk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Or an insatiably powerful enemy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Also a terrible risk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Either way, we can now ask about the ritual.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why don't you inform the room on how you utilized an entire race of people to break the ritual?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "One day then."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure you are. You are our errand boy. We are your vessel to what it is you want. So get busy, and do it quickly. After all, you're very powerful. But that changes day by day, doesn't it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So, Emon it is. Very well. Back in a day."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We will start figuring out and discussing a place for the final battle. We will find a place that is advantageous. Your input, if you feel the urge to give it, is always welcome. At least to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's going to be very difficult to pry the Cinder King from his nest."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Your supposed death will go far for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If anything, but I have no guarantees."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: As long as everything is on the up and up, and as long as we do not catch you in deep deceit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispers) Good luck with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We will make sure that the plan moves in the direction it is meant to.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Who? Those who are there, most likely. Here, I'll give you a written..." and she pulls out a small little side scroll pouch. Scribbling a note and says, "This is a decree from the council. They'll have to listen to you." She finishes writing it, rolls it up, "So they'll be at your disposal. It's not the army; it's the reserves."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's put one foot in front of the other first, shall we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go buy some black powder. This is the time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How do we get there? We don't know of any trees there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (as Victor) "No!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can scry.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ironically, any town where you go to buy black powder, it's always him. It's really interesting. I don't know why.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And he's in 16-bit every time! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we know anyone in Daxio? Can you scry there to find a tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can scry there regardless and find a tree, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does the rest of the council approve of this ridiculous plan?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I approve.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This non-plan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Allura says, "Yes. I mean, I don't know anyone in Daxio at the moment. Riskel was the only one. Or Riskel was the one I knew with a connection there, and his father, Mikael, was in Syngorn."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do you think that they will be agreeable to this plan?
